filename: How to give a great presentation.docx
relative_path: Guidelines/Research-related/Abstracts and presentations/How to give a great presentation.docx
full_text:
When planning a presentation there are some general things to consider

1. How long is your talk? - It is of 1st priority to stick to the allocated time. The shorter the presentation - the better it has to be and and more effort you need to put in preparing it!

2. What is your audience? - Think of who will be in the audience, and what is their background? For ovarian cancer researchers you dont need to explain ovarian cancer.

3. Add the correct Acknowledgements! This is of utmost importance - acknowledge everyone who have contributed and enabled your work, including the funding agencies.

4. Practice, practice, practice - ask friends, family members to listen to you present. Organise at least 1-2 rehearsal talks!

When you are making the presentation

1. Focus on LOGIC

Every slide should have one main message = title in a short sentence. Titles for "results" slides are either the main RESULT or CONCLUSION of data presented on your slide.

In general: 1-2 introductory slides, 80% of your slides should be on results, and 1-2 conclusion slides.

2. Focus on CLEARNESS

Use minimum amount of text/words - words instead of sentences

Avoid too busy slides - max 2 images per slide - > split slides, use animations

Every image and animation on your slides should have a reason to exist

3. Get feedback!
Send your slides to your project members, peers, and supervisor for comments.

Plan what to say and write down the script of your talk

1. Focus on LOGIC and CLEARNESS

• Think of 1-3 main messages you wish to convey for each slide

• Formulate these to 2-6 complete sentences – write them to the notes

• Sentences should be short and logical –avoid complicated words

2. Focus on MEANING

• What does this mean? Interpret your results and graphs - use whole sentences.

• Add 1-2 explanatory sentences to your main result slides

3. Focus on TRANSITIONS

• Think how and why each topic leads to the next step, and say this in your talk.
